message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "first insurance adds 10% surcharge per item"
Current error: Expected { premium: 115 }, received { premium: 105 }
Passing tests so far: 13

Test: `quote({customer:{yearsWithMHPCO:0}, firstInsurance: true, items:[sword]})` expects 115.
100 base + 10 (first insurance, +10% of policy base) + 5 fee = 115.

Implement: add optional `firstInsurance: boolean` to input. When true, add +10% surcharge on policy base (additive with other policy rates).

Note: spec says first insurance and loyalty are both policy-wide modifiers — they apply additively. So policyTotal = componentsTotal × (1 - loyalty + firstInsurance + ...).

Do NOT refactor. EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the test and implementation files, then make the minimal change to pass the failing test.
Now I'll make the minimal change to support `firstInsurance`.

content.text: Floating point precision issue. Let me fix by computing the surcharge as an addition rather than multiplying through a combined rate.

content.text:
All 14 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Added optional `firstInsurance: boolean` to quote input. When true, applies a +10% policy-level surcharge computed on `componentsTotal` and added to the loyalty-adjusted policy total.
- **Result**: All tests now pass (14 passing, 10 todo)
- **Approach**: Minimal — introduced `FIRST_INSURANCE_SURCHARGE_RATE = 0.1` and `firstInsuranceSurchargeRateFor` helper following the existing pattern. Computed the surcharge as a separate additive term (`componentsTotal * firstInsuranceSurchargeRate`) instead of merging it into the loyalty multiplier to avoid floating-point precision issues that produced `115.00000000000001`.

Files modified:
- `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_04-01-47_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts`
